source: Incus Server - 2 ZFS Setup: Loading ZFS and creating the storage pool

options: An open-source logical volume manager and filesystem (created by Sun for Solaris)
questions.why: ZFS combines a volume manager and filesystem; it originated at Sun Microsystems.
modprobe zfs loads the module; no output means success.

questions.q: modprobe zfs returns an error. What is the most likely cause?
options: UEFI secure boot is enabled (the module would need to be signed)
questions.why: Secure boot is the most likely culprit; it must be off for the unsigned module to load.

questions.q: Which command lists block devices so you can tell the OS disk from a free disk?
questions.why: lsblk shows disks, partitions and mountpoints (e.g. OS on /dev/sda, free /dev/sdb).

questions.q: Create a ZFS pool named 'storage' on the device /dev/sdb.
With several available disks, what does the book suggest for performance and redundancy?
questions.why: raidz gives better performance and redundancy (details are out of scope, but noted).

questions.q: After creating the pool, what should you do?
questions.why: Reboot again after creating the pool.
